Introduction.script: BERT is a bidirectional transformer model for language representation learning.
bullet_points: It uses both left and right context during pre-training.

Methodology.script: The model is pre-trained with masked language modeling and next sentence prediction before being fine-tuned on downstream tasks.
bullet_points: Pre-training happens on large unlabeled text corpora.

Results.script: The paper reports strong benchmark performance on tasks including GLUE and SQuAD.
bullet_points: Bidirectional pre-training improves many NLP tasks.

Discussion.script: A key idea is that a single pre-trained model can be adapted to many tasks with minimal task-specific changes.
bullet_points: Fine-tuning is simple compared with building separate task-specific models.

Conclusion.script: The paper argues that deeply bidirectional pre-training is an effective foundation for language understanding.
bullet_points: BERT became a strong baseline for later NLP systems.
